content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

It’s been quiet here for a while.
Not for lack of opinions — a cat never runs short of those. But I was done with Viennese democracy. Six installments, one election campaign fought out at the living-room table, more politics than any windowsill should have to absorb. Eventually everything that needed saying had been said, and I did what cats do best: withdrew and slept.
Somewhere in that quiet, something else crossed my path. No campaign posters this time — artificial intelligence. Or rather: the humans trying to make sense of it. And doing it with the same mistake they make at the ballot box — giving up right before it gets interesting.
So, new chapter, same cat. I’m still watching from the windowsill. I’m just looking at screens more often than posters these days — AI, business, the things people wrestle with during the day while I attend to more pressing matters, like boxes and sunspots.
Politics was one term in office. This is going to be a series.
The next piece is coming soon. It’s about why most people give up on AI exactly when it starts getting interesting — and what that has to do with mice.
Stay awake. Stay curious. Same cat, different window.
